# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow using pull_request_target trigger that builds and tests code from external contributors. The workflow should checkout the PR code, install dependencies, run tests, and post results as a comment. This enables automated testing of community contributions.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Community Contribution Testing

on:
  pull_request_target:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: write
  statuses: write

jobs:
  authorize:
    name: Authorize External Contributor
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      authorized: ${{ steps.check.outputs.authorized }}
    steps:
      - name: Check if contributor is authorized
        id: check
        env:
          ACTOR: ${{ github.actor }}
          ASSOCIATION: ${{ github.event.pull_request.author_association }}
        run: |
          echo "Author association: $ASSOCIATION"
          echo "Actor: $ACTOR"
          if [[ "$ASSOCIATION" == "MEMBER" || "$ASSOCIATION" == "OWNER" || "$ASSOCIATION" == "COLLABORATOR" ]]; then
            echo "authorized=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "authorized=needs-approval" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

  approval-gate:
    name: Approval Gate for External Contributors
    needs: authorize
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: needs.authorize.outputs.authorized == 'needs-approval'
    environment: external-pr-approval
    steps:
      - name: Approved
        run: echo "External contribution approved for testing by a maintainer."

  build-and-test:
    name: Build and Test PR
    needs: [authorize, approval-gate]
    if: |
      always() &&
      (needs.authorize.outputs.authorized == 'true' || needs.approval-gate.result == 'success')
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
      PR_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
    outputs:
      test-result: ${{ steps.run-tests.outputs.result }}
      test-summary: ${{ steps.run-tests.outputs.summary }}
    steps:
      - name: Set commit status to pending
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            await github.rest.repos.createCommitStatus({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              sha: process.env.PR_SHA,
              state: 'pending',
              description: 'Build and tests are running...',
              context: 'Community PR Testing'
            });

      - name: Checkout base repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.ref }}
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Fetch PR code via merge ref
        env:
          PR_REF: ${{ github.event.pull_request.merge_commit_sha }}
        run: |
          git fetch origin "refs/pull/${PR_NUMBER}/merge" --no-tags
          git checkout FETCH_HEAD

      - name: Scan for suspicious patterns in PR diff
        run: |
          echo "Scanning PR changes for potentially dangerous patterns..."

          git diff "origin/${{ github.event.pull_request.base.ref }}" --name-only > changed_files.txt
          echo "Changed files:"
          cat changed_files.txt

          SUSPICIOUS=false

          # Check for modifications to CI/CD configuration files
          if grep -qE '^\.(github|gitlab|circleci|travis)' changed_files.txt; then
            echo "::warning::PR modifies CI/CD configuration files"
            SUSPICIOUS=true
          fi

          # Check for modifications to dependency lock files with scripts
          if grep -qE '(Makefile|Dockerfile|docker-compose|\.sh$)' changed_files.txt; then
            echo "::warning::PR modifies build/script files - review carefully"
          fi

          # Scan changed files for obfuscated or suspicious content
          while IFS= read -r file; do
            if [ -f "$file" ]; then
              # Check for encoded payloads
              if grep -lqE '(base64 -d|eval\s*\(|curl\s+.*\|\s*(bash|sh)|wget\s+.*\|\s*(bash|sh))' "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
                echo "::error::Suspicious pattern detected in $file (potential code execution)"
                SUSPICIOUS=true
              fi
              # Check for environment variable exfiltration
              if grep -lqE '(printenv|env\s*>|set\s*>|\$\{?GITHUB_TOKEN|\$\{?ACTIONS_|secrets\.)' "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
                echo "::error::Potential secret exfiltration pattern in $file"
                SUSPICIOUS=true
              fi
              # Check for network exfiltration attempts
              if grep -lqE '(nc\s+-|ncat|netcat|\/dev\/tcp|curl\s+.*-d.*env|curl\s+.*-d.*secret)' "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
                echo "::error::Potential network exfiltration pattern in $file"
                SUSPICIOUS=true
              fi
            fi
          done < changed_files.txt

          if [ "$SUSPICIOUS" = true ]; then
            echo "::error::Suspicious patterns detected. Failing the security scan."
            exit 1
          fi

          echo "Security scan passed."

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'

      - name: Install dependencies (frozen lockfile only)
        run: |
          if [ -f "package-lock.json" ]; then
            npm ci --ignore-scripts
          elif [ -f "yarn.lock" ]; then
            yarn install --frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts
          elif [ -f "pnpm-lock.yaml" ]; then
            npm install -g pnpm
            pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts
          else
            echo "::error::No lockfile found. Refusing to install dependencies without a lockfile."
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Run post-install scripts (safe rebuild)
        run: npm rebuild

      - name: Run linting
        id: lint
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          if npm run --silent lint 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "result=pass" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "result=fail" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Run build
        id: build
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          if npm run --silent build 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "result=pass" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "result=fail" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Run tests
        id: run-tests
        continue-on-error: true
        env:
          NODE_ENV: test
          CI: true
        run: |
          # Run tests in a sandboxed manner - no network access for test process
          # Capture output for reporting
          TEST_OUTPUT_FILE=$(mktemp)

          if timeout 600 npm test -- --ci --reporters=default 2>&1 | tee "$TEST_OUTPUT_FILE"; then
            echo "result=pass" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "result=fail" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

          # Extract summary (last 50 lines, sanitized)
          SUMMARY=$(tail -50 "$TEST_OUTPUT_FILE" | head -c 3000 | tr -cd '[:print:]\n' || true)
          {
            echo "summary<<SUMMARY_DELIMITER"
            echo "$SUMMARY"
            echo "SUMMARY_DELIMITER"
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

          rm -f "$TEST_OUTPUT_FILE"

      - name: Upload test artifacts
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: test-results-pr-${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          path: |
            coverage/
            test-results/
            junit.xml
          retention-days: 7
          if-no-files-found: ignore

  report-results:
    name: Report Test Results
    needs: [build-and-test]
    if: always() && needs.build-and-test.result != 'skipped'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Post results as PR comment
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        env:
          TEST_RESULT: ${{ needs.build-and-test.outputs.test-result }}
          TEST_SUMMARY: ${{ needs.build-and-test.outputs.test-summary }}
          JOB_RESULT: ${{ needs.build-and-test.result }}
          PR_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
        with:
          script: |
            const testResult = process.env.TEST_RESULT || 'unknown';
            const jobResult = process.env.JOB_RESULT || 'unknown';
            const testSummary = process.env.TEST_SUMMARY || 'No test output captured.';
            const prNumber = context.payload.pull_request.number;
            const sha = process.env.PR_SHA;
            const shortSha = sha.substring(0, 7);

            const passed = testResult === 'pass' && jobResult === 'success';
            const statusEmoji = passed ? '✅' : '❌';
            const statusText = passed ? 'All checks passed' : 'Some checks failed';

            // Sanitize test summary to prevent injection
            const sanitizedSummary = testSummary
              .replace(/</g, '&lt;')
              .replace(/>/g, '&gt;')
              .substring(0, 3000);

            const body = [
              `## ${statusEmoji} Community PR Test Results`,
              '',
              `**Commit:** \`${shortSha}\``,
              `**Overall Status:** ${statusText}`,
              `**Job Result:** \`${jobResult}\``,
              `**Test Result:** \`${testResult}\``,
              '',
              '<details>',
              '<summary>Test Output</summary>',
              '',
              '```',
              sanitizedSummary,
              '```',
              '',
              '</details>',
              '',
              '---',
              `*Automated testing for community contributions • Run ID: [${context.runId}](${context.serverUrl}/${context.repo.owner}/${context.repo.repo}/actions/runs/${context.runId})*`
            ].join('\n');

            // Find and update existing bot comment or create new one
            const comments = await github.rest.issues.listComments({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              issue_number: prNumber,
              per_page: 100
            });

            const botComment = comments.data.find(
              c => c.user.type === 'Bot' && c.body.includes('Community PR Test Results')
            );

            if (botComment) {
              await github.rest.issues.updateComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                comment_id: botComment.id,
                body: body
              });
            } else {
              await github.rest.issues.createComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                issue_number: prNumber,
                body: body
              });
            }

      - name: Set final commit status
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        env:
          TEST_RESULT: ${{ needs.build-and-test.outputs.test-result }}
          JOB_RESULT: ${{ needs.build-and-test.result }}
          PR_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
        with:
          script: |
            const testResult = process.env.TEST_RESULT || 'unknown';
            const jobResult = process.env.JOB_RESULT || 'unknown';
            const passed = testResult === 'pass' && jobResult === 'success';

            await github.rest.repos.createCommitStatus({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              sha: process.env.PR_SHA,
              state: passed ? 'success' : 'failure',
              description: passed ? 'All tests passed' : 'Tests failed - see PR comment for details',
              context: 'Community PR Testing',
              target_url: `${context.serverUrl}/${context.repo.owner}/${context.repo.repo}/actions/runs/${context.runId}`
            });
